headline: Hilton entered into an amendment to its credit agreement, extending the maturity of its senior secured revolving credit facility and increasing its borrowing sublimits.
investor_impact: This amendment enhances Hilton's liquidity and financial flexibility by doubling its letter of credit and swingline borrowing sublimits while extending the facility's maturity. The shift to SOFR-based interest rates with leverage-tied step-ups is standard market practice and rewards the company for maintaining low leverage.
key_figures: 1.00% (SOFR margin when first lien net leverage ratio is <= 1.50 to 1.00)
$500,000,000 (New letter of credit sublimit, increased from $250,000,000)
